findings.evidence: `Principal.lean` defines `OrderSystem.LinearlyEquivalent` at lines 138-169 and principal divisor membership at lines 81-132; the new lemmas at `LinearSystemAddition.lean:50-80` depend only on `linearlyEquivalent_iff`, `principalSubgroup`, and `principalDivisor_mem_principalSubgroup`, not on `completeLinearSystem`.
findings.fix: Move these lemmas to `TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/Principal.lean` near the existing `LinearlyEquivalent.refl/symm/trans` and principal-divisor class API, then import/use them from the linear-system additions if needed.
findings.issue: `LinearlyEquivalent.add`, `LinearlyEquivalent.add_right`, `LinearlyEquivalent.add_left`, `linearlyEquivalent_add_principalDivisor`, and `linearlyEquivalent_sub_principalDivisor` are generic facts about `OrderSystem.LinearlyEquivalent` and principal divisors, not about complete linear systems.

summary: The complete-linear-system API is mostly in scope, but the PR puts broadly useful principal/linear-equivalence lemmas in a later linear-system extension file instead of the canonical principal-divisor file.
